confirmed.title: Unsupported sub-field-type note rendered as hardcoded English (repeater)
confirmed.detail:
Inside SubFieldInput's text/unknown fallback, the visible muted note `

type {type} not yet supported

` is hardcoded English. The component already imports `useArqelTranslations` (line 43), so a translator is available. This is user-visible chrome shown when a repeater row contains a field type not in SUPPORTED_TYPES. Not covered by the existing ledger entries for this file (those targeted per-block editor faces, not this fallback note).
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/repeater/RepeaterInput.tsx:334, inside SubFieldInput's text/unknown fallback: `

type {type} not yet supported

` — hardcoded English with no __() / translator. The module already imports useArqelTranslations (line 43) and uses it at line 393 in another component, so a translator is reachable; SubFieldInput is itself a React function component and could call the hook. This is user-visible muted chrome rendered when a repeater row holds a sub-field type not in SUPPORTED_TYPES. Not a duplicate: the two existing ledger entries for this file (round 7) target line 516 (drag/clone/remove/expand-collapse aria-labels) and line 633 (visible '+ Add item' text + 'Remove item N' aria-label). Neither touches the SubFieldInput unsupported-type note. The i18n loop has previously treated analogous empty-state/diagnostic strings as in-scope (e.g. WizardInput 'No wizard steps configured.'). Not vendored: located in fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a deliberately out-of-scope item. Genuinely reproduces, novel, user-facing. Confidence med because the string is a developer-leaning diagnostic ('not yet supported') rather than core end-user content, but it is still rendered English chrome and consistent with prior accepted findings.

confirmed.title: Unsupported sub-field-type note rendered as hardcoded English (builder)
confirmed.detail:
Same pattern as RepeaterInput: `

type {type} not yet supported

` is hardcoded visible English in the SubFieldInput text/unknown fallback. `useArqelTranslations` is imported (line 30) so t() is reachable. Distinct from the ledger BuilderInput entry (per-block visible face).
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at packages-js/fields-advanced/src/builder/BuilderInput.tsx:361: `

type {type} not yet supported

` — hardcoded visible English rendered inside the SubFieldInput component when a sub-field type is unsupported. This is genuinely user-facing (a muted-foreground paragraph shown under the input), not an aria-label. Ledger check: the file has six prior BuilderInput entries — R7 ("+ Add block" / "Remove block N"), and R15 (drag-handle, expand/collapse, move-up, move-down, clone-block aria-labels). None cover the "type {type} not yet supported" note. A grep for "not yet supported"/"not_supported" in the ledger returns nothing. So this string is novel. useArqelTranslations is imported (line 30) and used in two other components in the same file (lines 387, 498). SubFieldInput is itself a React function component (line 283), so t() is reachable by adding the hook call. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it is in fields-advanced/src/builder, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn). Not a deliberately out-of-scope item. confidence high.

confirmed.title: Unsupported sub-field-type note rendered as hardcoded English (wizard)
confirmed.detail:
Same hardcoded visible note `

type {type} not yet supported

` in WizardInput's SubFieldInput fallback. `useArqelTranslations` is imported (line 27). Not the same line/concern as the ledger WizardInput visible-face entry.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at WizardInput.tsx:276: `

type {type} not yet supported

` renders a visible, user-facing hardcoded English note in the SubFieldInput fallback. This is genuinely untranslated: SubFieldInput (lines 198-293) never calls useArqelTranslations — the hook is imported at line 27 but only consumed at line 304 in a separate component, so the translator is not even in scope here. The R1-R32 ledger has five WizardInput entries (lines 436, 474, 513, 584 — empty-state, step counter, per-step aria-label, sr-only progress, and the 'Back' nav-button visible face) but none cover line 276 or the 'not yet supported' note. Nearby ledger lines 271/274/277 belong to unrelated files (AiSelectInput, CommandPalette, vendored shadcn sidebar). Not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* (not vendored) and not a deliberately-out-of-scope item. Novel, real, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: Showcase E2E demo heading hardcoded English (GridFormDemo)
confirmed.detail:
`

Grid Form Demo

` is a hardcoded English visible heading in a showcase E2E demo page with no i18n infrastructure available in the app. Same caveat as VersionsDemo: borderline out-of-scope test scaffold, low severity.
confirmed.why_real:
Reproduces at apps/showcase/resources/js/Pages/GridFormDemo.tsx:64 — `

Grid Form Demo

` is a hardcoded English visible heading rendered on the live /admin/grid-form-demo route (registered in apps/showcase/app/Providers/AppServiceProvider.php:43). NOT a duplicate: the ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) has zero apps/showcase entries and no detection JSON references GridFormDemo/grid-form-demo. NOT vendored shadcn (it's an app page, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Precedent supports in-scope: the ledger already includes app-level pages (apps/demo, apps/marketplace Pages/Components were localized in prior rounds), so app pages are not categorically excluded. The finding's own rationale is factually wrong on a load-bearing point: it claims "no i18n infrastructure available in the app," but apps/showcase/resources/js/app.tsx imports and mounts I18nProvider + LocaleSwitcher from @arqel-dev/i18n — so t()/useI18n IS available here. A logged-in admin visiting the route sees untranslated English. Severity is low (E2E test scaffold fixture, login-gated, heading incidental to its responsive-reflow purpose; sibling synthetic "Field A".."Field F" labels are intentional placeholders), but it is a genuine, novel, user-facing hardcoded-string defect in an in-scope app with i18n infra present.

confirmed.title: DevTools InertiaInspector navigation timestamp still uses toLocaleTimeString() with no locale arg (ledger claims fixed, code unchanged)
confirmed.suggested_key: (self-contained devtools i18n: pass navigator.language-derived locale to toLocaleTimeString)
confirmed.detail: `{new Date(entry.timestamp).toLocaleTimeString()}` in the navigation-history list is called with no locale argument, so each navigation event's timestamp is formatted in the engine default locale rather than the panel's active locale (`navigator.language`). The ledger records this surface as fixed at InertiaInspector.tsx:145 ("DevTools InertiaInspector navigation timestamp uses toLocaleTimeString() with no locale arg — the exact pattern fixed in sibling TimeTravel"), but the call is still un-argumented and `git` shows no modification to the `toLocaleTimeString()` line. Same root cause and same un-applied/regressed fix as TimeTravel.tsx:106. Repro: browser language pt-BR, open Inertia inspector panel, navigate; the Navigation tab shows en-US 12h timestamps instead of pt-BR 24h. Fix: pass the BCP-47 locale resolved from navigator.language (as i18n.ts already does for translations).
confirmed.why_real: Verified against the actual code: InertiaInspector.tsx:148 still reads `{new Date(entry.timestamp).toLocaleTimeString()}` with no locale argument. The panel HAS a resolved active locale — i18n.ts:159 exports activeLocale() derived from navigator.language and uses it for all translations — so this timestamp is formatted in the JS engine default locale instead of the panel's active locale. Under navigator.language=pt-BR the Navigation tab shows en-US 12h timestamps rather than pt-BR 24h, a genuine user-facing i18n defect. Not vendored (devtools-extension, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). The sibling TimeTravel.tsx:106 carries the identical un-argumented pattern, confirming a shared, un-applied fix. The ledger DOES contain an entry for this exact surface (line 1371-1374, round 10) claiming it was fixed ('the exact pattern fixed in sibling TimeTravel'), but the code was never modified — the toLocaleTimeString() line is unchanged and still un-argumented. Because the recorded fix was never applied and the defect persists verbatim in shipping code, this is a false/regressed ledger entry, not a genuinely-resolved duplicate; the live defect remains actionable. is_duplicate=false because no actual fix exists in the codebase despite the ledger record.
